According to legend, it originated in [[:en:979|979 AD]] during the [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen era]] when a branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|qq=|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|qq=|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]], also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], Kazama Nobuaki, a [[:en:Southern Court|South Court (Japan)]] commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]], inflicting significant damage on the [[:en:Northern Court|North Court (Japan)]] and [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]. He is said to have been a disciple of [[:en:Nisshō|Nichishō]], one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|qq=|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|qq=|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to [[:en:Murakami Domain|Murakami Domain]], where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]] * [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata City]] == External Links == * {{ill|Samurai clans|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地=[[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}} {{ill|Kazama, Nagano|ja|風間 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama, Nagano|1=Kazama, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223456}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} |根拠地=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] {{ill|Kanbara District|ja|蒲原郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbara District|1=Kanbara district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621281}} {{ill|Sawakami Village|ja|沢海村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakami Village|1=Sōmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553213}}
[[:en:Iwafune District, Niigata|Iwashiro District]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]]
[[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Akumi District, Yamagata|Akumi District]] [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata, Yamagata]]
{{ill|Tagawa District|ja|田川郡 (羽前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa District|1=Tagawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576762}} [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]] |人物={{ill|Kazama Nobuaki|ja|風間信昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Nobuaki|1=Kazama Nobuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665641}}
{{ill|Kazama Shinichi|ja|風間眞一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665659}} {{ill|Kazama Kazuki|ja|風間一輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Kazuki|1=Ikki Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665635}} |支流= }} '''Kazama clan''' (風間氏) is one of the {{ill|Clans in Japan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clans in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, it originated in [[:en:979|979 AD]] during the [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen era]] when a branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]], also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], Kazama Nobuaki, a [[:en:Southern Court|South Court (Japan)]] commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]], inflicting significant damage on the [[:en:Northern Court|North Court (Japan)]] and [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]. He is said to have been a disciple of [[:en:Nisshō|Nichishō]], one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to [[:en:Murakami Domain|Murakami Domain]], where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]] * [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata City]] == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] – Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association * [http://www.tsuruokakanko.com/tsuruoka/paseo/spot2/heishindo.html Former Kazama Residence] – Tsuruoka Tourism Association {{translated page|ja|風間氏|version=104615436|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] {{ill|Tagawa District|ja|田川郡 (羽前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa District|1=Tagawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576762}} [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]] |人物={{ill|Kazama Nobuaki|ja|風間信昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Nobuaki|1=Kazama Nobuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665641}}
{{ill|Kazama Shinichi|ja|風間眞一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665659}} {{ill|Kazama Kazuki|ja|風間一輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Kazuki|1=Ikki Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665635}} |支流= }} '''Kazama clan''' (風間氏) is one of the {{ill|Clans in Japan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clans in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, it originated in [[:en:979|979 AD]] during the [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen era]] when a branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]], also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], Kazama Nobuaki, a [[:en:Southern Court|South Court (Japan)]] commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]], inflicting significant damage on the [[:en:Northern Court|North Court (Japan)]] and [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]. He is said to have been a disciple of [[:en:Nisshō|Nichishō]], one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to [[:en:Murakami Domain|Murakami Domain]], where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]] * [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata City]] == External Links == * {{ill|Samurai clans|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地=[[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}} {{ill|Kazama, Nagano|ja|風間 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama, Nagano|1=Kazama, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223456}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} |根拠地=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] {{ill|Kanbara District|ja|蒲原郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbara District|1=Kanbara district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621281}} {{ill|Sawakami Village|ja|沢海村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakami Village|1=Sōmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553213}}
[[:en:Iwafune District, Niigata|Iwashiro District]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]]
[[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Akumi District, Yamagata|Akumi District]] [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata, Yamagata]]
{{ill|Tagawa District|ja|田川郡 (羽前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa District|1=Tagawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576762}} [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]] |人物={{ill|Kazama Nobuaki|ja|風間信昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Nobuaki|1=Kazama Nobuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665641}}
{{ill|Kazama Shinichi|ja|風間眞一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665659}} {{ill|Kazama Kazuki|ja|風間一輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Kazuki|1=Ikki Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665635}} |支流= }} '''Kazama clan''' (風間氏) is one of the {{ill|Clans in Japan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clans in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, it originated in [[:en:979|979 AD]] during the [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen era]] when a branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]], also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], Kazama Nobuaki, a [[:en:Southern Court|South Court (Japan)]] commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]], inflicting significant damage on the [[:en:Northern Court|North Court (Japan)]] and [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]. He is said to have been a disciple of [[:en:Nisshō|Nichishō]], one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to [[:en:Murakami Domain|Murakami Domain]], where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]] * [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata City]] == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] – Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association * [http://www.tsuruokakanko.com/tsuruoka/paseo/spot2/heishindo.html Former Kazama Residence] – Tsuruoka Tourism Association {{translated page|ja|風間氏|version=104615436|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] {{ill|Samurai clans|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地=[[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}} {{ill|Kazama, Nagano|ja|風間 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama, Nagano|1=Kazama, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223456}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} |根拠地=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] {{ill|Kanbara District|ja|蒲原郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbara District|1=Kanbara district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621281}} {{ill|Sawakami Village|ja|沢海村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakami Village|1=Sōmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553213}}
[[:en:Iwafune District, Niigata|Iwashiro District]] [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami]]
[[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Akumi District, Yamagata|Akumi District]] [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata, Yamagata]]
{{ill|Tagawa District|ja|田川郡 (羽前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa District|1=Tagawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576762}} [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]] |人物={{ill|Kazama Nobuaki|ja|風間信昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Nobuaki|1=Kazama Nobuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665641}}
{{ill|Kazama Shinichi|ja|風間眞一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665659}} {{ill|Kazama Kazuki|ja|風間一輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Kazuki|1=Ikki Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665635}} |支流= }} '''Kazama clan''' (風間氏) is one of the {{ill|Clans in Japan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clans in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, it originated in [[:en:979|979 AD]] during the [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen era]] when a branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]], also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], Kazama Nobuaki, a [[:en:Southern Court|South Court (Japan)]] commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]], inflicting significant damage on the [[:en:Northern Court|North Court (Japan)]] and [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]. He is said to have been a disciple of [[:en:Nisshō|Nichishō]], one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to [[:en:Murakami Domain|Murakami Domain]], where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]] * [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata City]] == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] – Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association * [http://www.tsuruokakanko.com/tsuruoka/paseo/spot2/heishindo.html Former Kazama Residence] – Tsuruoka Tourism Association {{translated page|ja|風間氏|version=104615436|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] {{ill|Samurai clans|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地=[[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}} {{ill|Kazama, Nagano|ja|風間 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama, Nagano|1=Kazama, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223456}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} |根拠地=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] {{ill|Kanbara District|ja|蒲原郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbara District|1=Kanbara district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621281}} {{ill|Sawakami Village|ja|沢海村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakami Village|1=Sōmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553213}}

{{ill|Tagawa District|ja|田川郡 (羽前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa District|1=Tagawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576762}} [[:en:Tsuruoka|Tsuruoka]] |人物={{ill|Kazama Nobuaki|ja|風間信昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Nobuaki|1=Kazama Nobuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665641}}
{{ill|Kazama Shinichi|ja|風間眞一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665659}} {{ill|Kazama Kazuki|ja|風間一輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Kazuki|1=Ikki Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665635}} |支流= }} '''Kazama clan''' (風間氏) is one of the {{ill|Clans in Japan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clans in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, it originated in [[:en:979|979 AD]] during the [[:en:Tengen (era)|Tengen era]] when a branch of the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]], also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], Kazama Nobuaki, a [[:en:Southern Court|South Court (Japan)]] commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under [[:en:Nitta Yoshisada|Nitta Yoshisada]], inflicting significant damage on the [[:en:Northern Court|North Court (Japan)]] and [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]. He is said to have been a disciple of [[:en:Nisshō|Nichishō]], one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to [[:en:Murakami Domain|Murakami Domain]], where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]] * [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata City]] == External Links == * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/01hokusin/04nagano/17017.html Kazama Shrine] – Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association * [http://www.tsuruokakanko.com/tsuruoka/paseo/spot2/heishindo.html Former Kazama Residence] – Tsuruoka Tourism Association {{translated page|ja|風間氏|version=104615436|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:風間氏]] {{wikidata link|Q113207672}} {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama {{ill|Samurai clans|ja|武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samurai clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=buke|WD=Q2927809}} |出身地={{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}} {{ill|Kazama, Nagano|ja|風間 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama, Nagano|1=Kazama, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223456}}{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} |根拠地={{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}} {{ill|Kanbara District|ja|蒲原郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbara District|1=Kanbara district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621281}} {{ill|Sawakami Village|ja|沢海村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawakami Village|1=Sōmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553213}}
{{ill|Iwafune District, Niigata|en|Iwafune District, Niigata|WD=Q1155124|lt=Iwashiro District}} {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|en|Murakami, Niigata|WD=Q284503|lt=Murakami}}
{{ill|Dewa Province|en|Dewa Province|WD=Q876032|lt=Dewa Province}} {{ill|Akumi District, Yamagata|en|Akumi District, Yamagata|WD=Q1155592|lt=Akumi District}} {{ill|Sakata, Yamagata|en|Sakata, Yamagata|WD=Q504690|lt=Sakata, Yamagata}}
{{ill|Tagawa District|ja|田川郡 (羽前国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa District|1=Tagawa district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576762}} {{ill|Tsuruoka|en|Tsuruoka|WD=Q734416|lt=Tsuruoka}} |人物={{ill|Kazama Nobuaki|ja|風間信昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Nobuaki|1=Kazama Nobuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665641}}
{{ill|Kazama Shinichi|ja|風間眞一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665659}} {{ill|Kazama Kazuki|ja|風間一輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Kazuki|1=Ikki Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665635}} |支流= }} '''Kazama clan''' (風間氏) is one of the {{ill|Clans in Japan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clans in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, it originated in {{ill|979|en|979|WD=Q23784|lt=979 AD}} during the {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|WD=Q1327101|lt=Tengen era}} when a branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}, dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}}. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|WD=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}}, also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, Kazama Nobuaki, a {{ill|Southern Court|en|Southern Court|WD=Q3001082|lt=South Court (Japan)}} commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}}, inflicting significant damage on the {{ill|Northern Court|en|Northern Court|WD=Q1201819|lt=North Court (Japan)}} and {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji}}. He is said to have been a disciple of {{ill|Nisshō|en|Nisshō|WD=Q952593|lt=Nichishō}}, one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to {{ill|Murakami Domain|en|Murakami Domain|WD=Q849483|lt=Murakami Domain}}, where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to {{ill|Sakata, Yamagata|en|Sakata, Yamagata|WD=Q504690|lt=Sakata}}, marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}} * {{ill|Sakata, Yamagata|en|Sakata, Yamagata|WD=Q504690|lt=Sakata City}} == External Links == * Kazama Residence] – Tsuruoka Tourism Association {{translated page|ja|風間氏|version=104615436|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:風間氏]] {{wikidata link|Q113207672}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] According to legend, it originated in {{ill|979|en|979|WD=Q23784|lt=979 AD}} during the {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|WD=Q1327101|lt=Tengen era}} when a branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}, dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}}. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|WD=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}}, also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, Kazama Nobuaki, a {{ill|Southern Court|en|Southern Court|WD=Q3001082|lt=South Court (Japan)}} commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada}}, inflicting significant damage on the {{ill|Northern Court|en|Northern Court|WD=Q1201819|lt=North Court (Japan)}} and {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji}}. He is said to have been a disciple of {{ill|Nisshō|en|Nisshō|WD=Q952593|lt=Nichishō}}, one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to {{ill|Murakami Domain|en|Murakami Domain|WD=Q849483|lt=Murakami Domain}}, where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to {{ill|Sakata, Yamagata|en|Sakata, Yamagata|WD=Q504690|lt=Sakata}}, marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}} * {{ill|Sakata, Yamagata|en|Sakata, Yamagata|WD=Q504690|lt=Sakata City}} == External Links == * {{ill|Kazama Shinichi|ja|風間眞一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665659}} {{ill|Kazama Kazuki|ja|風間一輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Kazuki|1=Ikki Kazama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665635}} |支流= }} '''Kazama clan''' (風間氏) is one of the {{ill|Clans in Japan|ja|日本の氏族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clans in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, it originated in {{ill|979|en|979|WD=Q23784|lt=979 AD|1=979}} during the {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|WD=Q1327101|lt=Tengen era|1=Tengen (era)}} when a branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}}, dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. The clan is considered one of the {{ill|Jinke-to|ja|神家党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinke-to|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a lineage of Shinto priest families. == Niigata Prefecture == The Kazama clan became prominent as {{ill|Local lords|ja|土豪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local lords|1=dogō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423883}} in {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}. * They are said to trace their roots to {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|WD=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}}, also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, Kazama Nobuaki, a {{ill|Southern Court|en|Southern Court|WD=Q3001082|lt=South Court (Japan)|1=Southern Court}} commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|WD=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}}, inflicting significant damage on the {{ill|Northern Court|en|Northern Court|WD=Q1201819|lt=North Court (Japan)|1=Northern Court}} and {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}. He is said to have been a disciple of {{ill|Nisshō|en|Nisshō|WD=Q952593|lt=Nichishō|1=Nisshō}}, one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to {{ill|Murakami Domain|en|Murakami Domain|WD=Q849483|lt=Murakami Domain|1=Murakami Domain}}, where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama Hikosō|ja|風間彦惣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Hikosō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, moved to {{ill|Sakata, Yamagata|en|Sakata, Yamagata|WD=Q504690|lt=Sakata|1=Sakata, Yamagata}}, marking the establishment of the Sakata Kazama lineage. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}} * {{ill|Sakata, Yamagata|en|Sakata, According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|qid=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}}, also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|qid=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, Kazama Nobuaki, a {{ill|Southern Court|en|Southern Court|qid=Q3001082|lt=South Court (Japan)|1=Southern Court}} commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|qid=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}}, inflicting significant damage on the {{ill|Northern Court|en|Northern Court|qid=Q1201819|lt=North Court (Japan)|1=Northern Court}} and {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}. He is said to have been a disciple of {{ill|Nisshō|en|Nisshō|qid=Q952593|lt=Nichishō|1=Nisshō}}, one of the {{ill|Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|ja|六老僧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Murata Myōhōji|ja|妙法寺 (長岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata Myōhōji|1=Myōhō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11446951}}. === Murakami === The Murakami branch of the Kazama clan originated in {{ill|Niitsu, Sawakami|ja|新津沢海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niitsu, Sawakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but later moved to {{ill|Murakami Domain|en|Murakami Domain|qid=Q849483|lt=Murakami Domain|1=Murakami Domain}}, where they became prominent as textile merchants. == Yamagata Prefecture == === Sakata === In {{ill|1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja|享保10年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1725 (Kyōhō 10)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the third head of the Murakami Kazama family, {{ill|Kazama According to legend, it originated in {{ill|979|en|979|qid=Q23784|lt=979 AD|1=979}} during the {{ill|Tengen (era)|en|Tengen (era)|qid=Q1327101|lt=Tengen era|1=Tengen (era)}} when a branch of the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|qid=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}}, dispatched to serve at {{ill|Kazama Shrine|ja|風間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} {{ill|Minochi District|ja|水内郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minochi District|1=Minochi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11548296}}, adopted the surname Kazama. According to legend, {{ill|Kazama Tachibano|ja|風間橘五|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazama Tachibano|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} settled there while serving under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|qid=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}}, also known as Kiso Yoshinaka.{{Sfn|Ōta|1934|p=1457}} * During the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|qid=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, Kazama Nobuaki, a {{ill|Southern Court|en|Southern Court|qid=Q3001082|lt=South Court (Japan)|1=Southern Court}} commander and Shinano Governor, fought valiantly under {{ill|Nitta Yoshisada|en|Nitta Yoshisada|qid=Q1328796|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|1=Nitta Yoshisada}}, inflicting significant damage on the {{ill|Northern Court|en|Northern Court|qid=Q1201819|lt=North Court (Japan)|1=Northern Court}} and {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}. Nichiren|1=Six Elder Priests of Nichiren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392500}}, and founded temples like {{ill|Hama Tsuchihokkeji|ja|浜土法華寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama
